description: Outpatient (OP) referral conversion dataset. Each patient was advised admission for a procedure during an OPD visit. Coordinator follow-up calls track conversion. Visit history, vitals, labs, prescriptions, and call logs are raw inputs.

current_referral.op_advised_reason: 59-year-old male with stable angina, hypertension, diabetes, and dyslipidemia. Coronary angiogram pending as OP procedure; currently mildly symptomatic with dyspnea on exertion, hemodynamically stable by available findings.

evidence_text: complaint of mild dyspnea on exertion

visit_history.chief_complaint: Chest discomfort on exertion x 2 months, occasional sweating
visit_history.examination: S1 S2 normal, no murmurs. Bilateral basal crepts negative. No pedal edema.
visit_history.imaging: T wave inversion in II, III, aVF
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Stable angina, T2DM uncontrolled, dyslipidemia, HTN
visit_history.advice: TMT advised. Lifestyle modification. Review with reports.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Persistent chest discomfort, dyspnea climbing 1 flight stairs
visit_history.examination: TMT positive at stage II, ST depression 1.5mm in inferior leads. Stopped due to chest pain.
visit_history.imaging: T inversion II, III, aVF persisting
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Stable angina (TMT positive), advised CAG
visit_history.advice: Coronary angiogram strongly advised. OP admission counselled.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Mild dyspnea on exertion, no fresh angina episodes
visit_history.examination: Hemodynamically stable. CVS: S1 S2 normal. No raised JVP.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Stable angina, OP admission for CAG advised
prescription: Continue same. Hold metformin morning of procedure.
visit_history.advice: Admit for CAG. Pre-anesthesia checkup done. Patient counselled re: cost ~Rs 50,000.

call_history.transcript_summary: Patient said he wants to discuss with family. Mentioned cost is a concern. Asked about insurance coverage.
Patient confirmed not interested in proceeding here. Prefers to do procedure in his hometown hospital where his nephew is a doctor. Polite but firm.

advised_actions.title: OP Admission Advised
advised_actions.description: Patient recommended for OP admission based on surgical procedure

current_referral.op_advised_reason: 33-year-old male with chronic generalized dystonia due to kernicterus, currently being considered for bilateral pallidotomy as per neurology and neurosurgery advice. Procedure is not yet scheduled.

evidence_text: right UL parasthesias since last 3 months

visit_history.chief_complaint: Worsening involuntary movements, postural difficulty x 6 months
visit_history.examination: Generalized dystonic posturing, increased tone all 4 limbs, dystonic gait. MMSE not assessable.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Generalized dystonia secondary to kernicterus, GDD
visit_history.advice: Trial of medical management. Functional MRI advised.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Partial response to medications, persistent disability
visit_history.examination: Sustained dystonic posturing. BFM dystonia rating scale 42/120.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Refractory generalized dystonia, considering DBS / pallidotomy
visit_history.advice: Neurosurgery referral for bilateral pallidotomy evaluation.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Right upper limb tingling x 3 months, constipation
visit_history.examination: BFM 44/120, slight worsening. Right UL paresthesia, sensory normal otherwise.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Generalized dystonia + new R UL paresthesia + Vit B12 deficiency
visit_history.advice: B12 supplementation. Bilateral pallidotomy under evaluation, pending counselling.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Patient and family ready for surgical discussion
visit_history.examination: Stable. Family discussion ongoing.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Refractory generalized dystonia, OP admission for bilateral pallidotomy planning
visit_history.advice: Admit for pre-op workup. Cardiac, pulmonary clearance needed.

advised_actions.title: TO PLAN BILATERAL PALLIDOTOMY
advised_actions.description: Prescribed op admission: TO PLAN BILATERAL PALLIDOTOMY

advised_actions.title: R/A ONE MONTH
advised_actions.description: Prescribed follow up: R/A ONE MONTH

advised_actions.title: TO CONSULT DR SARKAR, NEURO SURGEON
advised_actions.description: Prescribed referral: TO CONSULT DR SARKAR, NEURO SURGEON

current_referral.op_advised_reason: 68-year-old male with advanced osteoarthritis, rotator cuff tear, and prior stroke, planned for arthroscopy, left knee replacement, and right shoulder reverse replacement. Pre-anaesthesia check-up and medical optimisation needed.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Sudden weakness right side, slurred speech (recovered 80%)
visit_history.examination: Right hemiparesis 4/5, dysarthria. NIHSS 4 at admission.
visit_history.imaging: MRI: Acute lacunar infarct left corona radiata
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Acute lacunar stroke, T2DM, HTN, dyslipidemia
visit_history.advice: Stroke rehabilitation. Strict BP/sugar control. F/U in 1 month.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Bilateral shoulder pain (R > L), left knee pain x several years
visit_history.examination: R shoulder: painful arc, Hawkins +ve. L knee: grade 4 OA, ROM 0-90 deg painful.
visit_history.imaging: MRI R shoulder: full thickness rotator cuff tear with retraction. X-ray L knee: grade 4 OA tricompartmental.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: R shoulder rotator cuff tear, L knee grade 4 OA, AC joint OA
visit_history.advice: Surgical opinion for L TKR, R reverse shoulder replacement, L knee arthroscopy.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Increasing pain, mobility very limited, patient agreeable to surgery
visit_history.examination: Same as before. Walking with stick. Sleep disturbed by pain.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Severe symptomatic L knee OA + R shoulder RCT, OP planned
visit_history.advice: Pre-anesthesia workup. Cardiac clearance (post-stroke). Insulin if needed perioperatively.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Came for pre-op clearance and admission planning
visit_history.examination: Same. Cardiac fitness obtained.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Multiple joint surgery planned: L knee arthroscopy + L TKR + R shoulder reverse arthroplasty (staged)
visit_history.advice: Stop aspirin 7 days pre-op. Cost discussion ongoing. Estimated stay 5-7 days.

call_history.transcript_summary: Patient said he is currently managing with physiotherapy and pain medications. Pain has decreased somewhat. Wants to delay surgery. Will consider if pain returns. Concerned about stroke history and surgical risk.

advised_actions.title: Procedure: Arthroscopy Left shoulder + supraspinatus repair + subacromial decompression + acromioplasty
advised_actions.description: Prescribed procedure: Arthroscopy Left shoulder + supraspinatus repair + subacromial decompression + acromioplasty

advised_actions.title: Procedure: Right shoulder Reverse replacement
advised_actions.description: Prescribed procedure: Right shoulder Reverse replacement

advised_actions.title: Arthroscopy Left shoulder + supraspinatus repair + subacromial decompression + acromioplasty
advised_actions.description: Prescribed op admission: Arthroscopy Left shoulder + supraspinatus repair + subacromial decompression + acromioplasty

current_referral.op_advised_reason: 22-year-old male with recurrent right orbital pain, vomiting, and status migrainosus. IV analgesics started and strong recommendation for ward admission. MRI pituitary advised for further evaluation.

evidence_text: recurrent right orbital pain since 1 month

visit_history.chief_complaint: Recurrent right side headache x 3 months, throbbing, with redness/tearing
visit_history.examination: Neuro exam normal between attacks. CN intact. No papilledema.
visit_history.imaging: MRI brain plain: normal. Sinuses normal.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Cluster headache (R)
visit_history.advice: Headache diary. Avoid triggers. F/U if no improvement.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Headaches more frequent, now with nausea and vomiting, sleep disturbed
visit_history.examination: Exam normal. Patient distressed.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Cluster + migraine overlap, refractory
visit_history.advice: MRI pituitary advised. Prophylaxis trial.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Severe continuous headache x 4 days with persistent vomiting, unable to take orals, URI symptoms
visit_history.examination: Dehydrated, tearfulness, photophobia. Throat congested. CN intact, no focal deficit.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Status migrainosus with cluster headache and URI
visit_history.advice: Strong recommendation for OP admission for IV management and MRI pituitary. Patient and family agree initially.

call_history.transcript_summary: Patient interested in admission, pending consultation with parents and insurance pre-authorization.
Insurance does not cover migraine admission as deemed not medically necessary by TPA. Patient lacks funds. Declining admission. Will manage as OPD with IV in day care if needed.

advised_actions.title: MRI DYNAMIC PITUTAARY SEQUENCES
advised_actions.description: Prescribed radiology test: MRI DYNAMIC PITUTAARY SEQUENCES

advised_actions.title: Admission to ward
advised_actions.description: Prescribed op admission: Admission to ward

current_referral.op_advised_reason: 44-year-old female with MRI-confirmed right knee medial meniscus tear; planned for right knee arthroscopic medial meniscus repair with explicit OP admission advised by orthopaedics consultant.

visit_history.chief_complaint: R knee pain x 4 months after slip, worse with stairs
visit_history.examination: R knee: medial joint line tenderness, McMurray +ve, no effusion. ROM full but painful.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: R knee medial meniscus injury (clinical)
visit_history.advice: MRI R knee. Physiotherapy. Avoid squatting.

visit_history.chief_complaint: MRI report review, persistent pain despite PT
visit_history.examination: Same findings. Mild quadriceps wasting.
visit_history.imaging: MRI: Grade 3 medial meniscus tear posterior horn, mild ACL strain. Cartilage intact.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: R knee medial meniscus tear grade 3
visit_history.advice: Arthroscopic meniscal repair vs partial meniscectomy. Counselled about OP admission.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Decision for surgery, no improvement from PT
visit_history.examination: Same.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: R knee medial meniscus tear, planned arthroscopic repair
visit_history.advice: OP admission for arthroscopic medial meniscus repair. Day-care or 1-night stay.

advised_actions.title: OP Admission Advised
advised_actions.description: Patient recommended for OP admission based on surgical procedure

advised_actions.title: MINOR SURGICAL PROFILE
advised_actions.description: Prescribed lab test: MINOR SURGICAL PROFILE

advised_actions.title: Procedure: RIGHT KNEE ARTHROSCOPIC MEDIAL MENISCUS REPAIR
advised_actions.description: Prescribed procedure: RIGHT KNEE ARTHROSCOPIC MEDIAL MENISCUS REPAIR

current_referral.op_advised_reason: 44-year-old male with large atrial septal defect, moderate-to-severe pulmonary arterial hypertension, and chronic kidney disease on dialysis. Plan for percutaneous ASD device closure with outpatient admission advised.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Routine pre-dialysis review
visit_history.examination: AV fistula left forearm, thrill +ve. Mild pedal edema.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: ESRD on MHD thrice weekly, secondary HTN, anemia of CKD
visit_history.advice: Continue MHD MWF. Strict fluid restriction.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Increasing exertional dyspnea, ankle swelling beyond usual
visit_history.examination: Loud P2, parasternal heave, fixed split S2. Clubbing absent.
visit_history.imaging: 2D Echo: Large secundum ASD 24mm, L to R shunt, RV dilated, RVSP 62mmHg, moderate-severe PAH
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Large secundum ASD with moderate-severe PAH on background of ESRD on MHD
visit_history.advice: ASD device closure feasibility evaluation. Cardiac MRI advised. Multidisciplinary review.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Dyspnea NYHA III, dialysis tolerance reduced
visit_history.examination: Same. RV heave.
visit_history.imaging: Cardiac MRI: ASD 24mm, Qp:Qs 2.3, RV volumes increased, suitable for percutaneous closure if rim adequate.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Large ASD with PAH, suitable for device closure, ESRD on MHD
visit_history.advice: Percutaneous ASD device closure planned. High-risk procedure given PAH and ESRD. Counselled.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Final pre-procedure visit, has questions about cost
visit_history.examination: Stable.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Awaiting OP admission for ASD device closure
visit_history.advice: Admit. Coordinated dialysis on day before. Cost ~Rs 3.5 lakhs counselled.

call_history.transcript_summary: Patient very keen but cost is a major issue. Asked if any discount or scheme available. Has CGHS but coverage uncertain for device. Family wants to admit.
Follow-up call. Patient said still trying to arrange funds, has approached relatives. CGHS approval pending. Asked for one more week.

advised_actions.title: Percutaneous ASD device closure
advised_actions.description: Prescribed op admission: Percutaneous ASD device closure

advised_actions.title: Procedure: Percutaneous ASD device closure
advised_actions.description: Prescribed procedure: Percutaneous ASD device closure

current_referral.op_advised_reason: 62-year-old female with symptomatic gall-stone disease (planned cholecystectomy), erosive and mild chronic gastritis, hypertension, diabetes, hypothyroid, and anemia. Cholecystectomy and colonoscopy are planned; GI surgery consult advised.

symptoms: Pain radiating to back and right shoulder
evidence_text: Pain radiating to back and right shoulder
Patient reports excess gas formation (30 years)

visit_history.chief_complaint: Long-standing gas/indigestion, recent RUQ pain, weight loss 3kg
visit_history.examination: Mild tenderness epigastrium. Murphy's sign equivocal. No icterus.
visit_history.imaging: USG abdomen: Multiple gallstones, GB wall normal. No CBD dilatation.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Symptomatic cholelithiasis, IDA, hypothyroid (sub-optimal), DM, HTN
visit_history.advice: GI surgery referral. Optimize Hb, TFT before surgery. Endoscopy advised.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Endoscopy review, persistent symptoms
visit_history.examination: Pale, no fresh tenderness.
visit_history.imaging: UGI Endoscopy: erosive gastritis antrum, mild chronic gastritis. H pylori +ve.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Erosive + chronic gastritis (H pylori +ve), cholelithiasis, IDA improving
prescription: Triple therapy x 14 days (Pantop + Amox + Clarithro)
visit_history.advice: Cholecystectomy after H pylori eradication. Colonoscopy advised given anemia + age.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Post H pylori treatment, episodic RUQ pain radiating to back x 1 week
visit_history.examination: Mild RUQ tenderness, Murphy's negative. No jaundice.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Symptomatic cholelithiasis post H pylori treatment, ready for surgery
visit_history.advice: Lap cholecystectomy + colonoscopy planned. GI surgery to admit.

call_history.transcript_summary: Patient says she will visit hospital next month to discuss surgery with GI consultant directly. Currently visiting daughter in Bangalore. Will call back on return.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Palpitations on exertion, occasional chest tightness, fatigue
visit_history.examination: Soft systolic murmur at apex. Otherwise unremarkable.
visit_history.imaging: ECG: Sinus tachycardia, no ST changes
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Palpitations for evaluation, mild renal dysfunction NYD
visit_history.advice: Echo, Holter, USG KUB. Cardiology + Nephrology referrals.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Worsening palpitations, presyncope episode 3 days back
visit_history.examination: Same murmur. CVS exam otherwise unremarkable.
visit_history.imaging: Echo: LVEF 55%, mild MVP with mild MR. Holter: 18% PVC burden, occasional NSVT runs.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Symptomatic ventricular ectopy, MVP, mild renal dysfunction
visit_history.advice: EP study advised. Nephrology workup ongoing. Possible RFA candidate.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Pre-EP study workup, nephrology clearance pending
visit_history.examination: Stable.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Awaiting EP study, nephrology clearance needed first
visit_history.advice: Admit for EP study + RFA. Need nephro clearance for contrast/sedation.

call_history.transcript_summary: Patient busy at work, asked to call later. Nephrology clearance noted as required before admission.
Patient said advised by us to obtain nephrology clearance first. He has appointment with nephro next week. Will revert after that.
Brief call - no new updates. Nephro appointment pushed by 10 days due to consultant leave. Will update when seen.

advised_actions.title: Admission Advised
advised_actions.description: Patient recommended for OP admission based on clinical assessment

visit_history.chief_complaint: Bilateral knee pain, worse with activity, mobility reduced
visit_history.examination: Bilateral knee crepitus, varus deformity, ROM 0-100. Walking with stick.
visit_history.imaging: X-ray bilateral knees: grade 3-4 OA with osteophytes, joint space narrowing, varus alignment.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Bilateral knee OA grade 3-4, age-related, mild renal impairment
visit_history.advice: Conservative trial. Knee replacement option discussed for future. Avoid NSAIDs given renal function.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Pain about same, walking distance reduced to 100m, fall once at home
visit_history.examination: Same findings. Quadriceps wasting +.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Symptomatic bilateral knee OA, deteriorating, fall risk
visit_history.advice: TKR option re-discussed. Patient deferring.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Family came to ask about TKR, patient ambivalent
visit_history.examination: Same. ASA Grade 2-3.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: Bilateral knee OA, family-driven OP discussion
visit_history.advice: Pre-anaesthesia clearance needed. Cardiac evaluation given age. Renal precautions.

call_history.transcript_summary: Patient said still has pain but is doing physiotherapy. Plans to come in after a few months. Wants to delay surgery.
Patient continuing physiotherapy, mild pain, reports pain gradually decreasing. Not keen on surgery currently. Family supportive of patient's choice.
Stable, no change. Will revert if pain worsens. Asked us not to call frequently.

advised_actions.description: Prescribed procedure: Left total knee replacement

advised_actions.title: MAJOR SURGICAL PROFILE
advised_actions.description: Prescribed lab test: MAJOR SURGICAL PROFILE

visit_history.chief_complaint: Pain and swelling left ankle x 1 month after football injury
visit_history.examination: L ankle: mild swelling, tenderness lateral malleolus, ATFL stress test +ve, ROM mildly painful.
visit_history.imaging: X-ray L ankle: no fracture. MRI: partial tear ATFL, mild bone bruise.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: L ankle grade 2 lateral ligament sprain, ATFL partial tear
visit_history.advice: Conservative management. Physiotherapy. Surgery only if instability persists.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Persistent giving way of L ankle, recurrent twisting episodes
visit_history.examination: Anterior drawer +ve, talar tilt mild +ve. Functional instability.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: L ankle chronic instability post ATFL injury
visit_history.advice: If instability persists, ATFL repair (arthroscopic) OP procedure can be considered.

visit_history.chief_complaint: Family came for surgical decision
visit_history.examination: Same.
visit_history.diagnosis_text: L ankle chronic instability, OP arthroscopic ligament repair option
visit_history.advice: Surgery vs continued bracing discussed. Family wants to think.

call_history.transcript_summary: Spoke to patient's father (attendant). Said patient has opted for conservative management. Continuing PT. Will return only if instability worsens significantly. Family not keen on surgery for minor.

advised_actions.title: Admission Advised
advised_actions.description: Patient recommended for OP admission based on clinical assessment

advised_actions.title: Procedure: LEFT CLAVICLE ORIF WITH PLATING
